$comment: Intent-router routing table (ADR-0016). Array order IS priority: first matching route wins, compliance first, specific before general. Patterns are case-insensitive JS regex sources matched against the user prompt. The router injects ONE one-line hint; it never blocks, never auto-invokes. Keep patterns high-precision — a missed match costs nothing (descriptions still route), a wrong match mis-steers.

routes.hint: process the unsubscribe/DNC end-to-end (suppress, record, confirm); never draft a sales reply to an opt-out.
disposition the inbound reply — classify, pick channel, execute; read the full thread first.
turn the transcript into CRM updates + a recap draft + account memory in one flow.
score the completed call against the methodology with quoted coaching moments.
convert the call into a MEDDPICC capture, a proposed CRM update, and a follow-up draft.
build the pre-meeting brief — attendees, history, MEDDPICC gaps to probe, talk track.
pick the next touch for a silent prospect — value-add, breakup, or snooze; reads the thread first.
triage the net-new inbound within its SLA — score, route, draft the first response.
classify the inbox into priority classes and draft replies (draft-only).
compose the rundown — today's meetings, overdue promises, deal alerts, focus list.
batch on-ramp — triage -> research -> draft -> four gates + adversarial reviewer -> ONE review-pack -> gated send; use this instead of hand-rolling a batch loop.
draft or pressure-test the first-touch message against the personalization quality gate.
analyze sequence/variant conversion and recommend promote or retire.
build a compliant multi-touch cadence — every step draft-only ahead of the send gate.
prep the call block — openers, gatekeeper handling, voicemail scripts, per-dial logging.
run find -> ICP-score -> warm path -> draft as one orchestrated flow.
detect buying/timing triggers across accounts and map each to a play.
the reconciled account picture — CRM vs memory vs ledgers, every field labeled source + last-verified.
run the full A-Z on ONE named target — parallel research fan-out + a sequenced, multi-channel plan of attack (who first, channel, proof, CTA, dates); draft-only behind the gate.
build a deep, sourced account brief — HubSpot history first, then web.
fill record gaps from wired enrichment MCPs (web fallback) — provenance-labeled, applied via crm-operator.

patterns: \bwho else should i be talking to\b
routes.hint: map the buying committee — economic buyer, champion, blockers, engagement.
widen the deal to committee coverage with warm, role-specific drafts.
reframe the objection, draft the response, and map the MEDDPICC gap behind it.
portfolio-level MEDDPICC completeness across the pipeline population.
manager deep-dive — parallel risk/finance/competition lenses into a go-deeper list.
MEDDPICC health check — red/amber/green per element, gaps to dated actions.
measure how accurate past forecast calls were — variance and bias by rep.
run the HubSpot data-health scan — required fields, stale records, logging compliance.
build the period roll-up — commit/best-case weighted by MEDDPICC risk.
sweep the pipeline for stale deals, missing next steps, and close-date pushes.
portfolio retention health — NRR/GRR, churn, at-risk renewals, expansion rate.
single-account renewal health check, churn-save play, and expansion hypothesis.
route the non-standard term through formal approval before it reaches the customer.
prep the commercial exchange — concessions ladder, BATNA, procurement tactics.
run the pricing math — tiers, ramps, discounts, and approval routing.
build the seller's backward date-plan from signature through every gate.
build/update the shared buyer+seller milestone plan (MAP).
answer the RFP/questionnaire from approved answers; flag unanswerables to a human SME.
track the legal/procurement artifacts — MSA, DPA, order form — status and blockers.
define the POC — mutual success criteria, go/no-go gate, owners on both sides.
build the demo storyline tied to discovered pain with per-stakeholder moments.
build the ROI/value model from buyer-stated metrics and approved proof.
assemble the structured proposal with proof matched to decision criteria.
match approved reference customers and coordinate the intro logistics.
pull approved differentiation, traps, and rebuttals for the named competitor.
propose times, send the invite, confirm, or run no-show recovery via Calendar.
produce the structured SDR->AE / AE->CS handoff with completeness checks.
mine closed-deal patterns by segment/competitor/reason, or debrief one deal.
assemble the QBR narrative; metric math defers to sales-reporting.
run the RevOps metric rollup — funnel, coverage, attainment, scorecards.
check whether ramped headcount covers the quota at the required multiple.
plan account distribution, territory coverage gaps, and rebalancing.
apply routing logic — territory, segment, capacity — to assign ownership.
detect duplicates and recommend the surviving record before merging.
build the structured coaching brief — pipeline health, activity, call patterns.
per-rep activity + logging-compliance scorecard (a coaching input).
build the 30/60/90 ramp plan with certification checkpoints and ramped targets.
build the rep VOICE PROFILE from real sent messages so drafts sound like you.
route the dropped file into the right ESCC layer; claims stay reviewed candidates.
retrieve approved product facts — the single source for claims in drafts.
pull an approved exemplar or proven structure to anchor the draft.
review, approve, promote, or evolve learned instincts.
